Recommendation: Remove this test. Previously flagged in the Story 3.10 validation report — still present.' Journal step_end for review-tests confirms 're-flagged for removal' but no fix applied. observations.summary: The prepare-tests step created TDD red-phase scaffolding (adding skipped test blocks to existing files) but did not update the story file's tasks to reflect the completed scaffolding — the story's Tasks 7-10 said 'Add a describe block' but the describe blocks were already added by prepare-tests, forcing validate-story-2 to catch and fix the contradiction; 2nd occurrence (3.8, 3.11), confirming the hypothesis from ledger entry 38 with a broader scope than originally hypothesized.
observations.evidence: Story file _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-11-run-concurrent-conversations.md:420 — Decision (DP-2): 'Story Tasks 7-10 + Testing Requirements contradicted the actual test-file state — the story instructed the dev to "Add a describe block" and "add findMany/delete to the mock," but all four test files already contain complete it.skip'd Story 3.11 test scaffolding (TDD red phase) and the mockPrisma.conversation mock already has findMany/delete (lines 51-52). Chose amend-the-spec: updated Tasks 7-10 to "Activate the existing it.skip'd block".' Journal step_end for prepare-tests: '35 red-phase test scaffolds created across 5 files' (added to existing files, not new stub files). Journal step_end for validate-story-2 confirms the fix. Ledger entry 38 (Story 3.8) hypothesized: 'this likely recurs on any story where prepare-tests creates stub files for TDD red-phase compilation' — the scope is broader: it recurs whenever prepare-tests creates any scaffolding (new files OR additions to existing files), not just new stub files. Ledger entries 44 (3.9) and 50 (3.10) deduced non-recurrence because the narrow scope condition (creates new stub files) was not met.

observations.summary: The unit-tests step's opencode runner timed out (rc:124) during the initial run, output was salvaged (mid-stream narration about the test runner being killed), and the step was classified as 'success' despite no evidence of completion — 2nd occurrence (3.9 review-code, 3.11 unit-tests); the work was likely covered by implement-story (which already ran all 873 tests) and review-tests (which confirmed 873 pass), but the unit-tests step itself did not complete its validation.

Let me use setsid to start it in a new session so it survives.' No completion message. Ledger entry 41 (Story 3.9) records 1st occurrence: review-code timed out (rc:124), salvaged mid-stream narration, classified as success despite no code review completion.

observations.summary: Prior confirmed observation (ledger entries 5, 37, 43, 52 — Stories 2.2, 3.8, 3.9, 3.10) — the pattern did NOT recur in 3.11: the 3.9 update_step (requiring re-read of project-context.md and verification of each pattern before completing) is working, as implement-story proactively applied all applicable NFR patterns; the 5 NFR findings from review-nfrs are extensions beyond documented patterns or pre-existing patterns, not gaps in applying documented patterns.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for implement-story completion notes (story file line 443): 'NFR patterns verified: env-configured IIFE, findFirst tenant-scoped, select projection, error envelope, try/catch around JSON.parse, circuit breaker timer cleanup, in-process state cleanup in finally, raw resource body on success, @User() + auth guards, jest.advanceTimersByTimeAsync under fake timers, no console.log/any/@ts-ignore.' Story file NFR Evidence Audit (lines 521-527): 5 NFR findings — (1) MEDIUM missing take limit on countActiveConversations findMany (no take-limit pattern documented in project-context.md); (2) MEDIUM no timeout on DELETE fetch in handleRetry (project-context.md rule names GitHub API calls, not internal API calls — 'all other internal fetch calls also lack timeouts — pre-existing pattern, not introduced by 3.11'); (3) LOW missing select on conversation.create (project-context.md rule names findFirst/findUnique explicitly, not create); (4) LOW missing select on conversation.delete (same — not documented for delete); (5) LOW missing timing regression guard (no timing-test pattern documented for this method type). AC-4 NOT deferred: browser mock covers DELETE-before-POST ordering; 2 E2E scaffolds created.' AC-1 and AC-3 are backend-internal and E2E-deferred per DP-5 (same rationale as Stories 3.9/3.10).' Ledger entry 33 (Story 3.7) records the original observation; ledger entry 35 (Story 3.7) records the applied update_step to prepare-tests.

amendments.prompt: run Validate mode. Flag skipped story-related tests for un-skipping or removal (with reason). Fix stale transitional markers directly wherever found during the search, including those from earlier stories in the same directories — comments/headers claiming tests are skipped/disabled/red-phase when they're actually active must be updated to reflect current state. Do not defer out-of-scope markers to a separate validation. Treat empty placeholder test stubs (active tests with no assertions, only a comment or empty body) as transitional artifacts — remove them directly wherever found during the search, including those from earlier stories in the same directories, since an empty test that passes trivially inflates the count without verifying behavior.
amendments.rationale: The review-tests step has flagged but not removed an empty placeholder test stub in 2 stories (3.10, 3.11); the prompt covers skipped tests and stale comments/headers but not empty placeholder stubs (active tests with no assertions), so the agent classified it as a recommendation rather than a fix-directly item.

amendments.prompt: Create tests. After applying TDD red-phase scaffolding (adding skipped test blocks to new or existing files, adding test seams, or creating stub files), update the story file's tasks to reflect what was already done — tasks that instruct the dev to create scaffolding that prepare-tests has already applied should be amended to instruct activation of the existing scaffolding instead, so the story does not contradict the codebase state. Before deferring E2E coverage, verify no browser-level mock pattern can simulate the scenario — only defer if no mock covers the ACs, and record the check in the ATDD checklist. When creating regression guards for code that executes external commands with user-controlled input, apply a uniform guard template to every call site: exercise both credential-isolation invariants (no credentials leak via command arguments or environment variables) and input-injection invariants (malicious input is safely quoted and cannot alter the command's behavior). For any decision that arises, consult @_bmad-output/decision-policy.md before escalating: if a rule covers it, decide and continue. HALT only for decisions no rule covers.
amendments.rationale: The prepare-tests step has created scaffolding without updating the story file's tasks in 2 stories (3.8, 3.11), forcing validate-story-2 to catch and fix the contradiction; directing the agent to update the story file after applying scaffolding is a low-risk prompt tuning that prevents the drift.
